title: hieracium glaucinum subsp. chlorotephrinum
intro: Hieracium glaucinum subsp. chlorotephrinum, also known as the yellow hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Hieracium glaucinum subsp. chlorotephrinum include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its potential to help manage digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
